element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to identify colors and match groups of three or more
Player learns trajectory control and wall bouncing mechanics
Player adapts to unpredictable firing colors drawn from remaining on-screen colors

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After several shots (frequency varies by color count)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns time pressure increases as ceiling descends; connected bubbles fall when support is removed
Auditory stress signal indicating imminent game over condition
Hard failure boundary; crossing triggers game over

typical_relief_triggers: Successful three-bubble match removes bubbles and points awarded
Clearing entire arena advances to next level
Cascading matches (bubbles falling and creating new matches)
Solving a difficult arrangement without ceiling drop
typical_tension_triggers: Random color mismatch forcing suboptimal play
Fewer remaining color options increasing random firing difficulty
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating pressure loop: each successful volley brings ceiling closer; music accelerates; player forced into faster decision-making with fewer color options; relief comes only through clearing matching groups and arena completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Simple bubble arrangements with abundant matching opportunities; player learns pointer aiming and color matching fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasingly complex bubble placements requiring strategic shot planning; ceiling drops more frequent; color variety increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dense arena configurations with limited optimal moves; high ceiling drop frequency; minimal color redundancy forces precise play; arcade version culminates at level 30
notable_spikes: Transition from early simple patterns to mid-game complexity as level progresses
Levels where few color options remain in arena, constraining firing flexibility
Final levels in arcade 30-level sequence with deliberately constrained match opportunities

notable_level_patterns.description: Each round presents a new prearranged bubble pattern; patterns become increasingly packed and complex, reducing clear paths and forcing multi-step solution sequences
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early levels feature sparse, easily-matched clusters; later levels present interlocking structures requiring cascades

notable_level_patterns.description: Arcade version spans 30 levels; as player progresses, fewer distinct colors remain in arena, making random color generation a harder constraint—fired bubble color may not match available targets
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late arcade levels with 2-3 color options create high-tension scenarios where wrong shots are more likely

notable_level_patterns.description: After every few shots, ceiling descends; shot count between drops influenced by remaining bubble colors. Fewer colors = more frequent drops. Creates escalating time pressure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels implement this mechanic with varying intensity based on complexity

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor: Bust-A-Move SNES)
